g.text: Read it as inpainting in motion: VACE preserves the unmasked pixels exactly and fills the masked region with new content that is consistent with what's kept (and with the text prompt). Swap the mask/control
and the same machinery does pose-to-video, depth-to-video, outpainting, first-frame control, etc.
VACE branch turns it into 15 hints (figs 9,10) that steer the main DiT (fig 11).

The mask marks a region to REGENERATE (here, the sun) while the rest is KEPT. keep = video·(1−mask) and edit = video·mask are VAE-encoded (16+16) and stacked with the patchified mask (64) → a 96-ch control (fig 8). The
Wan2.1-VACE-1.3B · 12 · VACE, illustrated — keep one region, regenerate another, guided by control
